key_insight: A fixed-budget, cluster-aware evaluation protocol can materially change which RAG methods appear to win, showing that measurement choices can overstate progress in LLM-as-a-judge benchmarking.
engineering_relevance: For computational science and materials AI, the paper argues that retrieval systems should be compared under tightly controlled budgets and clustered statistical tests to avoid misleading conclusions about model quality.
method: Fix the top-100 candidate pool, evidence budget, answer cap, generator, and prompt.
Use pre-registered hypotheses plus cluster-aware inference and an exact cluster sign-flip check when feasible.
Replicate judgments with a second judge to reduce single-evaluator bias.
evidence_signal: On 400 multi-hop questions across CS/ML and Materials Science, a simple binomial test made all four semantic-baseline comparisons look significant, but cluster-aware inference reduced this to only one Bonferroni-significant result.
watch_item: Results depend on the fixed budget and clustered benchmark design, so the standard may be less informative if the evaluation setup or judge behavior changes substantially.
paper_title: A Fixed-Budget, Cluster-Aware Standard for LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation: A Multi-Hop RAG Stress Test
